Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json

Class JsonSerializer

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.JsonSerializer
All Implemented Interfaces:
JsonMetaProvider, ArrayRecordWritable, RecordWritable, TokenWritable
Direct Known Subclasses:
JcsSerializer, Json5Serializer, JsonlSerializer, JsonSchemaSerializer, JsonSerializer.Readable
public class JsonSerializer extends WriterSerializer implements JsonMetaProvider, TokenWritable, ArrayRecordWritable
Serializes POJO models to RFC 8259 JSON.

Produces strict JSON with double-quoted strings by default. For JSON5-style output (single quotes, etc.), use Json5Serializer.

Media types

Handles Accept types: application/json, text/json

Produces Content-Type types: application/json

Description

The conversion is as follows...

  • Maps (e.g. HashMaps, TreeMaps) are converted to JSON objects.
  • Collections (e.g. HashSets, LinkedLists) and Java arrays are converted to JSON arrays.
  • Strings are converted to JSON strings.
  • Numbers (e.g. Integer, Long, Double) are converted to JSON numbers.
  • Booleans are converted to JSON booleans.
  • nulls are converted to JSON nulls.
  • arrays are converted to JSON arrays.
  • beans are converted to JSON objects.

The types above are considered "JSON-primitive" object types. Any non-JSON-primitive object types are transformed into JSON-primitive object types through ObjectSwaps associated through the MarshallingContext.Builder.swaps(Class...) method. Several default transforms are provided for transforming Dates, Enums, Iterators, etc...

This serializer provides several serialization options. Typically, one of the predefined DEFAULT serializers will be sufficient. However, custom serializers can be constructed to fine-tune behavior.

Behavior-specific subclasses

The following direct subclasses are provided for convenience:

  • Json5Serializer - Default serializer, single quotes, simple mode.
  • Json5Serializer.Readable - Default serializer, single quotes, simple mode, with whitespace.
Example:

// Use one of the default serializers to serialize a POJO String json = JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(someObject); // Create a custom serializer for lax syntax using single quote characters JsonSerializer serializer = JsonSerializer.create().simple().sq().build(); // Clone an existing serializer and modify it to use single-quotes serializer = JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.copy().sq().build(); // Serialize a POJO to JSON String json = serializer.serialize(someObject);

Example output (Map of name/age):

{"name":"Alice","age":30}

Complex (nested object + array):

{"name":"Alice","age":30,"address":{"street":"123 Main St","city":"Boston","state":"MA"},"tags":["a","b","c"]}

Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
See Also:
  • JSON Basics
  • Json5Serializer - For JSON5-style output (single quotes, etc.)

    • copy

      public JsonSerializer.Builder<?> copy()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Creates a builder from this context object.

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      Overrides:
      copy in class WriterSerializer
      Returns:
      A new Builder object.

    • createSession

      public JsonSerializerSession.Builder<?> createSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class WriterSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • getSchemaSerializer

      public JsonSchemaSerializer getSchemaSerializer()
      Returns the schema serializer based on the settings of this serializer.

      Note that this method creates a builder initialized to all default settings, whereas copy() copies the settings of the object called on.

      Returns:
      The schema serializer.

    • getSession

      public JsonSerializerSession getSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Returns a session to use for this context.

      Note that subclasses may opt to return a reusable non-modifiable session.

      Overrides:
      getSession in class WriterSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session object.

    • serializeTokens

      public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator that opens a JsonTokenWriter over the output using default session arguments (mirrors Serializer.serialize(Object, Object)).

      The real implementation lives on JsonSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object). Callers that need request-derived configuration should call createSession() and invoke JsonSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object) on the built session instead.

      Specified by:
      serializeTokens in interface TokenWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output. Accepts Writer, OutputStream, File, or StringBuilder.
      Returns:
      A new JsonTokenWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.

    • isAddBeanTypes

      protected final boolean isAddBeanTypes()
      Add "_type" properties when needed.
      Overrides:
      isAddBeanTypes in class Serializer
      Returns:
      true if "_type" properties will be added to beans if their type cannot be inferred through reflection.
      See Also:
      • JsonSerializer.Builder.addBeanTypesJson()

    • isEscapeSolidus

      protected final boolean isEscapeSolidus()
      Prefix solidus '/' characters with escapes.
      Returns:
      true if solidus (e.g. slash) characters should be escaped.
      See Also:
      • JsonSerializer.Builder.escapeSolidus()

    • serializeArrayRecords

      public RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the streaming array-element RecordWriter (uses default session args; see serializeTokens(Object)). Real impl on JsonSerializerSession.serializeArrayRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      serializeArrayRecords in interface ArrayRecordWritable
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new element-streamed RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
